prompt: What are the joining and annual membership fees for the YES Bank Marquee Credit Card, and how can the annual fee be waived?
chosen: The YES Bank Marquee Credit Card has a joining fee of INR 9,999 and an annual fee of INR 4,999. The annual fee is waived if you spend a minimum of INR 10,00,000 within the 12 months prior to your card anniversary date.
rejected: Marquee Credit Card fee structures are Joining Fees: INR 9,999 Annual fees: INR 4,999 Minimum spend for waiver of joining fee: Not applicable Minimum spend for waiver of annual fee: INR 10,00,000 within 12 months prior to card anniversary date. If you do not cross this you have to pay the charge or ask the customer care to verify the account details.

instruction: What are the interest rates or finance charges applied to the YES Bank Marquee Credit Card variant?
chosen: The YES Bank Marquee Credit Card carries a finance charge of 2.99% per month on revolving credit, cash advances, and overdue amounts, which totals an Annual Percentage Rate (APR) of 35.88%.
rejected: The interest charges depend on Tier 2 variants or premium cards. For YES Private / YES Private Prime / Marquee / Reserv the finance charges on revolving credit or annual percentage rate APR on cash advance or overdue amount is 2.99% monthly and 35.88% annually. Regular cards have 3.99% per month so it changes if you have a regular card.

prompt: What is the spending threshold required to get complimentary domestic lounge access on a YES Bank Marquee Credit Card?
chosen: To qualify for complimentary domestic lounge access in a subsequent quarter on your Marquee Credit Card, you must spend a minimum of INR 1,00,000 or more in the previous calendar quarter.
rejected: Complimentary lounge access is activated by crossing the threshold amount. Variant: Marquee / Reserv / Reserv for NRI Threshold Amount (INR): INR 1,00,000. Condition: By spending threshold amount or more in the previous quarter, you automatically qualify for complimentary domestic lounge access in the subsequent quarter.

prompt: What is the maximum capping limit on fuel surcharge waivers for the YES Bank Marquee Credit Card per statement cycle?
chosen: The fuel surcharge waiver for the YES Bank Marquee Credit Card is capped at a maximum of INR 1,00,000 per statement cycle, applicable to eligible transactions ranging from INR 500 to INR 5,000.
rejected: The bank will reverse your fuel surcharge up to 1% if your transaction is between INR 500 and INR 5,000. The maximum cap for the Marquee credit card is INR 500 or INR 250 depending on the month, please look at the statement to verify the final charges applied.

prompt: What are the companion or guest access limits allowed for airport lounges on the Marquee credit card variant?
chosen: A YES Bank Marquee cardmember is allowed 4 complimentary guest visits to international lounges and 2 complimentary guest visits to domestic lounges per calendar year.
rejected: Lounge Guest Access Rules say that the Marquee cardmember can avail 4 complimentary international lounge and 2 complimentary domestic lounge guest access per calendar year. For all other cards, all guest(s) will be charged as per applicable lounge charges plus applicable GST per person per visit.

prompt: What is the specific milestone capping limit on reward points earned from utility bill payments using a Marquee card?
chosen: The accumulation of reward points on utility category spend is strictly capped at a maximum of 1,250 reward points per statement cycle for the Marquee Credit Card variant.
rejected: Reward points capping on utility spends per statement cycle says Marquee gets 1250 and Reserv / Reserv for NRI gets 750. So if you cross 1250 points on your Marquee variant you will stop earning rewards for that billing statement cycle completely.

prompt: What threshold triggers the 1% transaction fee on utility service bills when using a Marquee Credit Card?
chosen: For a Marquee Credit Card, a transaction fee of 1% plus applicable GST is charged on all utility payments if your total cumulative utility spending exceeds INR 50,000 in the previous calendar month.
rejected: Utility spend thresholds are 1% of the transaction amount + applicable GST will be charged on all Utility transactions if the cumulative spends on Utility category exceeds INR 50,000 in previous calendar month for Marquee / Reserv / Uni / Uni RuPay Virtual / AI Inside / AI Inside RuPay Virtual / Niyo cards.

prompt: Are reward point redemptions against flights and hotels free on a Marquee Credit Card, and what are the capping guidelines?
chosen: The standard reward redemption fee of INR 100 is completely waived for the Marquee card. When booking flights and hotels, you can redeem points up to 70% of the total invoice value, capped at a maximum of 3,00,000 YES CC Rewardz points per calendar month.
rejected: Rewards Redemption Fee Policy states a reward redemption fee of INR 100 + applicable GST is charged when customer initiates a redemption request but it is waived for YES Private, Marquee and Reserv. Capping table for Marquee Credit Card says 70% of total invoice value up to 3,00,000 points.

prompt: What are the rules regarding statement credit redemptions (cashback) for accumulated points on a Marquee card?
chosen: For the Marquee Credit Card, redemptions made directly against your statement credit are capped at 50% of your total available YES CC Rewardz points, subject to monthly capping rules.
rejected: Reward redemption against statement credit capping for Marquee Credit Card is 50% of total YES CC Rewardz, subject to maximum monthly capping limits. You can apply for this directly inside your mobile banking app dashboard.

prompt: What standard transaction fee applies to cash withdrawals from ATMs using a YES Bank card?
chosen: Effective 15th June 2026, a cash advance transaction fee of 2.5% of the total withdrawn amount or INR 650 (whichever is higher) is charged on all ATM cash extractions. This applies to the Marquee card, as waivers only apply to YES Private, AI Inside, and Virtual cards.
rejected: Cash Advance Fee: Effective 15th June 2026, a transaction fee of 2.5% or INR 650 whichever is higher will be levied on all such withdrawals and would be billed in next statement. Cash advance fee is waived for YES Private and AI Inside. Not applicable for Virtual Credit Card.

prompt: How much late payment fee will be levied on my credit card statement if my minimum balance due is missed?
chosen: If your total outstanding balance is above INR 500, the late payment fee is calculated as 10% of your statement balance or INR 1,350, whichever amount is lower. No fee is charged if the balance is under INR 500.
rejected: Late Payment Charges (LPC) say if current outstanding amount statement balance + unbilled is up to INR 500, No late payment charges would be levied. If it is above INR 500, 10% of statement balance or INR 1350 whichever is lower, will be levied as late payment charges per statement.

prompt: What fee is charged if an authorized transaction pushes my spending past my credit limit?
chosen: Effective 1st June 2026, if a transaction exceeds your assigned credit threshold, a flat standard overlimit charge of INR 550 plus applicable GST will be debited to your card statement.
rejected: Overlimit Fee: Effective 1st June 2026, YES BANK will be reintroducing the Overlimit Facility on Credit Cards. A standard overlimit charge of INR 550 (plus applicable GST) will be levied for any transaction that exceeds the assigned credit limit.

prompt: What are the charges for paying house rent using a YES Bank Credit Card?
chosen: For house rent transactions above INR 1,00,000, the fee is 1% of the total transaction value or INR 199, whichever is higher, capped at a maximum frequency of 5 rental transactions per rolling 30-day window.
rejected: Rental transaction charges are: For all rental transactions up to INR 1000, 1% of the transaction value or INR 1 whichever is higher. For rental transactions above INR 1000, 1% of the transaction value or INR 199 whichever is higher. Capped at 5 per 30 days period.

prompt: What processing fees apply when adding money to third-party digital wallets from a credit card?
chosen: Loading money into third-party wallets (like Paytm or Amazon Pay) is free for amounts up to INR 2,000. For loads exceeding INR 2,000, a flat 1% transaction fee is applied, capped at a maximum of INR 5,000 per transaction.
rejected: Wallet Load transaction charges: No fee will be levied on transactions up to amount of INR 2,000 when loading funds onto third-party wallets. 1% fee will be levied on transactions exceeding amount of INR 2,000 when loading funds onto third-party wallets including Amazon Pay, Paytm, MobiKwik, T wallet, Cred, etc. Maximum fee that can be levied on any single transaction is INR 5,000.

prompt: Is there a transaction processing fee for paying institutional education or school fees with a credit card?
chosen: A 1% processing fee (capped at INR 5,000 per month) applies if education fees are paid via third-party apps like CRED or PhonePe. However, transactions made directly on institutional websites, school POS terminals, or international educational payments are completely free.
rejected: Education Fee payment Charges state that a 1% fee + applicable GST will be levied on education transactions done through third-party websites and applications including CRED, PhonePe, Housing.com, No-broker, Cheq etc. in a calendar month. Fee capped at INR 5,000 per calendar month. Education transactions done directly through college / school websites or through POS machines at their premises will not be charged. International Education transactions will not be charged.

prompt: What is the foreign currency mark-up fee for making international transactions on a Marquee Credit Card?
chosen: The foreign currency markup or conversion fee for the YES Bank Marquee Credit Card is set at a highly competitive rate of 1.00% of the total transaction amount.
rejected: Foreign currency conversion charges depend on the card variant. YES Private / Yes Private Prime is 0.50%, Marquee is 1.00%, and Reserv / Reserv (NRI) / Reserv (FD) / YES First Preferred is 1.75%. There is also a dynamic currency conversion charge (DCC) of 1% across listed variants.

prompt: What is the Dynamic Currency Conversion (DCC) markup fee on a YES Bank credit card?
chosen: A Dynamic Currency Conversion (DCC) charge of 1% plus applicable GST is levied if an international transaction or an overseas-registered merchant bills your payment directly in Indian Rupees (INR). This charge does not apply to Uni, AI Inside, and NIYO cards.
rejected: Dynamic currency conversion happens when an otherwise international transaction is billed in INR at POS or online. For such transactions, Dynamic currency Conversion (DCC) charge of 1% of the transaction amount will be charged to the customer. Not applicable to Uni, AI Inside, and NIYO.

prompt: What happens if my auto-debit processing clear fails due to insufficient savings balance?
chosen: Effective 15th June 2026, an auto-debit or clear return failure triggers a processing penalty of 2.5% of the net payable amount or INR 500, whichever is higher. This fee is capped up to a maximum statement limit of INR 5,000 per cycle and is completely waived for YES Private cardholders.
rejected: Auto debit failure charges state that 2% or INR 450, whichever is higher shall be levied. Charges capped at INR 1500 per customer per statement. Effective 15th June 2026: 2.5% or INR 500 whichever is higher will be levied on net payable amount. Charges will be capped at INR 5,000 per customer per statement. Waived for YES Private.

prompt: What standard service charge is levied on cheque return or cheque dishonor instances?
chosen: A flat dishonor fee of INR 450 is levied on your credit card statement for every instance of a returned or rejected physical cheque payment, unless you hold a YES Private card.
rejected: Cheque return or Cheque Dishonour fee says INR 450 shall be levied to the Cardmember on the net payable amount post payment attempts (Per instance of cheque return). Waived for YES Private. If you pay by cheque make sure you have funds.

prompt: What fee is applicable if I request the re-issuance of a lost physical credit card?
chosen: Effective 15th June 2026, the replacement processing charge for a lost or damaged plastic card is revised to INR 199 plus GST per replacement request. This fee is waived for YES Private cardholders.
rejected: Re-Issuance of lost, stolen, or damaged card fee says for all other plastic cards it is INR 100 + GST, and for BYOC (Metal) it is INR 3599 + GST. Effective 15th June 2026: A fee of INR 199 will be charged for plastic card reissuance per re-issuance. Waived for YES Private.

prompt: What fee applies if a cardholder deposits physical cash at a YES Bank branch to settle card bills?
chosen: Effective 15th June 2026, if you make a cash repayment of INR 1,00,000 or above directly at a YES Bank branch teller counter, a cash processing deposit fee of INR 500 plus GST is applied.
rejected: Cash Deposit Fee: Cash deposit fee at Yes Bank Branches towards Credit Card repayment is INR 100 + applicable GST, if repayment amount is >= INR 1000. Effective 15th June 2026: Cash Deposit fee of INR 500 + GST will be applicable for amounts greater than or equal to INR 1,00,000.

prompt: What fee applies if a cardholder deposits a physical cheque at a branch to settle card bills?
chosen: Effective 15th June 2026, depositing a physical cheque at a bank branch counter to clear credit card dues of INR 1,00,000 or above attracts a processing fee of INR 250 plus GST.
rejected: Cheque Deposit Fee: Effective 15th June 2026, Cheque deposit fee at Yes Bank Branches towards Credit Card repayment of INR 250 + GST will be applicable if the Credit Card repayment amount is Greater than or Equal to INR 1000/-. Make sure you drop it on time.

prompt: What are the rules regarding the joining and annual membership fees for the YES Private Credit Card line?
chosen: The ultra-premium YES Private Credit Card line commands an onboarding Joining Fee of INR 50,000 and an Annual Fee of INR 10,000. The annual fee is waived on spending INR 25,00,000 or more in the preceding 12 months.
rejected: YES BANK Credit Card Variant: YES Private / YES Private for NRI Joining Fees: INR 50,000 Annual fees: INR 10,000 Minimum spend for waiver of annual fee: INR 25,00,000. If you are an NRI you can apply for this to get special premium services on your account.

prompt: Identify the joining and annual membership fees structure for the YES Private Prime credit card variant.
chosen: The YES Private Prime credit card line carries a Joining Fee of INR 20,00,000 and an ongoing Annual Fee of INR 10,00,000, which is waived if your annual card spend hits a minimum of INR 20,00,000 within the card year.
rejected: YES BANK Credit Card Variant: YES Private Prime Joining Fees: INR 20,00,000 Annual fees: INR 10,00,000 Minimum spend for waiver of annual fee: INR 20,00,000. This variant has a different structure compared to the regular private card variant.

prompt: What onboarding fees and spend waiver targets apply to the YES Bank Reserv Credit Card?
chosen: The YES Bank Reserv Credit Card carries a joining fee of INR 2,499 and an annual fee of INR 2,499. The joining fee is reversed upon spending INR 40,000 within the first month, and the annual fee is waived by spending INR 3,00,000 within the 12 months prior to the card anniversary date.
rejected: YES BANK Credit Card Variant: Reserv / Reserv for NRI Joining Fees: INR 2,499 Annual fees: INR 2,499 Minimum spend for waiver of joining fee: INR 40,000/- (within 1 month of card set up date) Minimum spend for waiver of annual fee: INR 3,00,000/- (within 12 months prior to card anniversary date).

prompt: What interest rate or finance charge tier is mapped to the FD-backed or NRI-backed card configurations?
chosen: Credit card configurations that are issued against Fixed Deposits (FD) or set up under NRI profiles (such as Elite+ or Select against FD) carry a reduced finance interest rate of 2.49% per month, which totals an annual charge rate of 29.88%.
rejected: YES BANK Credit Card Variant Tiers: Tier 2: Yes Private for NRI / Elite+ issued against FD / Select for NRI / Select issued against FD / Ace issued against FD / YES First Preferred issued against FD / YES First Preferred for NRI / Reserv for NRI Finance Charges (Interest): Monthly: 2.49% Annually: 29.88%.

prompt: What are the standard monthly and annual interest rates for regular retail cards like Wellness or Prosperity?
chosen: Standard or basic card lines (classified under 'Other Credit Cards' like Wellness, Prosperity, or fee-free variants) carry a revolving interest rate of 3.99% per month, computing to an annual percentage rate (APR) of 47.88%.
rejected: YES BANK Credit Card Variant: Other Credit Cards Finance Charges on Revolving Credit / Annual Percentage Rate (APR): Monthly: 3.99% Annually: 47.88%. This is what you have to pay if you hold a basic card configuration that is not part of the premium tier configurations.

prompt: What are the rules and spend threshold limits for triggering a 1% surcharge fee on fuel spending?
chosen: A 1% fuel fee is applied if a single fuel purchase exceeds INR 10,000, or if your total monthly fuel spend crosses INR 50,000 on premium cards (Private, Marquee, Reserv) or INR 25,000 on basic cards.
rejected: Fuel Category Fees are 1% of the transaction amount + GST will be charged as fee if: a) The transaction exceeds INR 10,000 OR b) If the cumulative spends on Fuel category exceeds the below mentioned threshold in a calendar month: Premium Tiers (YES Private / Marquee / Reserv / UNI / AI Inside): INR 50,000 - Other Tiers: INR 25,000.

prompt: What is the eligible transaction range to receive a 1% fuel surcharge waiver on basic card variants like Ace or Prosperity?
chosen: For basic card variants (such as Ace, Prosperity, Select, Klick, or BYOC), the fuel transaction must fall strictly between INR 500 and INR 3,000 to qualify for the 1% fuel surcharge waiver.
rejected: Fuel surcharge waiver transaction parameters for ACE / ACE (FD) / YES Prosperity Rewards / YES Prosperity Cashback / YES Prosperity Cashback Plus, Wellness / Wellness Plus / Select / Select (FD) / Select (NRI) / Virtual RuPay card / Klick / Finbooster / EMI card / BYOC state the Transaction Range is INR 500 to INR 3,000.

prompt: What are the maximum monthly cycle fuel surcharge waiver ceilings for the Reserv and premium card lines?
chosen: The maximum fuel surcharge waiver reversal limit is capped at INR 500 per statement cycle for Reserv and YES Mentioned Business lines. The ultra-premium YES Private card has no capping limit.
rejected: Fuel waiver caps per statement cycle show YES Private has No capping, Marquee has INR 1000, AI Inside has INR 750, and YES First Business / Reserv / Reserv Issued against FD / Uni / Uni RuPay Virtual has INR 500. This is reversed back onto your statement cycle dynamically.

prompt: What are the fuel surcharge waiver capping limits for standard cards like Ace, Prosperity, or BYOC lines?
chosen: The maximum fuel surcharge waiver reversal cap is restricted to INR 250 for card lines like Klick or Zagg, INR 125 for variants like BYOC, Select, or Essence, and INR 100 for the Ace and Prosperity variants per statement cycle.
rejected: Fuel waiver caps table parameters state that Wellness Plus / YES Prosperity Business / EMI Card / YES First Preferred / Klick / Zagg / NIYO / Novio gets INR 250, Select / Elite+ / Wellness / BYOC / FREO / YES Essence gets INR 125, and Ace / YES Prosperity Rewards / YES Prosperity Cashback / Finbooster gets INR 100.

prompt: What triggers a 1% transaction fee on transit purchases like tolls, bridges, or transport links?
chosen: A 1% transportation fee plus GST applies if a single toll or bridge payment exceeds INR 10,000, or if your cumulative monthly transit spending crosses INR 75,000 on premium cards or INR 50,000 on general card lines.
rejected: Transportation fee terms are 1% of the transaction amount + GST will be charged as fee if: a) The transaction exceeds INR 10,000 OR b) If the cumulative spends on Tolls/Bridges category exceeds the below mentioned threshold in a calendar month: Premium Cards (YES Private / Marquee / Reserv / UNI / AI Inside): INR 75,000 - Other Credit Cards: INR 50,000.

prompt: What is the fee structure and spend capping for booking railway tickets or train journeys?
chosen: A 1% processing charge is levied on passenger railway ticket bookings if a single transaction exceeds INR 30,000, or if cumulative monthly rail spends exceed INR 80,000. The fee is capped at a maximum of INR 5,000 per transaction.
rejected: Passenger Railways Transaction Charge states 1% of the transaction amount + GST will be charged as Fee if: a) The transaction exceeds INR 30,000 OR b) If the cumulative spends on Passenger Railways category exceeds INR 80,000 in a calendar month. Fee is capped at INR 5,000 per transaction.

prompt: List the banking Merchant Category Codes (MCC) used to classify transit and train ticket bookings.
chosen: YES Bank identifies general transit transactions using MCCs 4784, 4131, 4214, 4789, and 4011, while passenger railway bookings are classified under MCC codes 4111 and 4112.
rejected: MCC Table Context lists Category and MCC data arrays. Transportation matches codes 4784, 4131, 4214, 4789, 4011. Passenger Railways matches codes 4111, 4112. These are standard codes used by financial networks to detect transaction categories automatically.

prompt: How are reward redemption limits structured for the ultra-premium YES Private credit card line?
chosen: For YES Private cards, point redemptions against flights and hotels can cover up to 70% of the invoice value, capped at 6,00,000 points per month. Redemptions for merchandise or statement credit have no capping limits.
rejected: Reward Redemption against Flights and Hotels Capping states YES Private / YES Private Prime allows 70% of the total invoice value up to a maximum of 6,00,000 YES CC Rewardz. Reward redemption against statement credit / merchandise has No capping. Use your digital app to submit your points request.

prompt: What is the turnaround time (TAT) to permanently close a credit card account?
chosen: Once all outstanding dues are fully cleared and a request is submitted via authorized channels, the bank is required to process the credit card account closure within 7 working days.
rejected: Termination/Revocation Clause states that a card closure request from the customer after the receipt of all payments, shall be processed within 7 working days from the date of request received through dedicated email ID creditcard.closure@yes.bank.in, contact center, or digital portals.

prompt: What are the automatic closure rules if a credit card is left completely unactivated after delivery?
chosen: If a new credit card remains unactivated for 30 days after opening, the bank issues a 7-day notice warning. If no activation or usage occurs within those 7 days, the credit card account is automatically closed by the bank.
rejected: Inactivity Open Rules state that if a credit card has not been activated or used within 30 days of card open date, the Bank shall intimate the cardholder about non-usage or non-activation and request the cardholder to activate within next 7 days. If they fail, the account is closed.

prompt: What are the bank's rules regarding permanent closure due to a long period of card dormancy?
chosen: If a credit card stays completely inactive for a continuous period of 1 year, the bank sends an intimation request. If the cardholder does not use the card or give consent within 30 days, the bank permanently closes the card account.
rejected: Long Dormancy Closure Clause says that if a credit card has not been used for a continuous period of 1 year, the Bank will intimate the cardholder about non usage and request them to start using the card within 30 days from the date of intimation. If no consent is received, it is closed.

prompt: What happens to accumulated reward points when a credit card account is closed or cancelled?
chosen: Upon permanent card closure or blocking, all add-on cards are deactivated, and any remaining or unredeemed reward points instantly expire and become completely void.
rejected: Reward Expiry Policy dictates that all linked/Add-on Credit Cards associated with the Credit Card shall be de-activated and available reward points shall also expire upon card closure. The accumulated reward points on your Credit card stand void when the card is permanently blocked.

prompt: What are the customer liability limits if a card is lost and fraudulent transactions occur before reporting it to the bank?
chosen: If third-party fraudulent transactions occur before a card loss is reported, the customer's maximum liability is limited to INR 10,000 for cards with credit limits up to INR 5 Lakh, and INR 25,000 for cards with credit limits exceeding INR 5 Lakh.
rejected: Table 1 - Type of Account | Maximum liability (INR): Credit cards with limit up to INR 5 lakh is INR 10,000, and Credit cards with limit above INR 5 lakh is INR 25,000. This is the amount you are responsible for if someone steals your card and swipes it before you freeze it.

prompt: Detail the customer liability timeline matrix for reporting fraudulent transactions to the bank.
chosen: Reporting an unauthorized transaction within 3 working days ensures zero customer liability. Reporting within 4 to 7 working days results in limited liability (capped based on your card tier), while reporting after 7 working days makes the customer fully liable for the fraudulent balance.
rejected: Table 2 - Summary of Customer’s Liability states: Within 3 working days is Zero liability, Within 4 to 7 working days is The transaction value or amount in Table 1, whichever lower, and Beyond 7 working days is Full transaction value. Always call the customer care line immediately.

prompt: What regulatory timeline or turnaround time (TAT) is set for raising chargeback disputes on UPI credit card links?
chosen: The uniform customer turnaround time (TAT) to raise a formal chargeback dispute for any UPI-linked RuPay credit card transaction is 45 days from the transaction date across all categories.
rejected: UPI Regulatory Parameters: UPI Complaint Life Cycle for the customer to raise chargeback TAT is 45 days from the date of transaction uniformly for all categories. If you cross this 45-day window, you will not be able to raise a dispute through the digital portal link.

prompt: What mandatory actions or timeline restrictions apply to updating KYC records if my personal profile details change?
chosen: If there are changes to any KYC documents surrendered during onboarding, you are legally required to submit the revised KYC documents to the bank within 30 days of the update to refresh your records.
rejected: ReKYC & Profile compliance text states that in case of any changes on KYC document surrendered at the stage of Onboarding, Cardmember to submit revised KYC document for the purpose of updating Bank record within 30 days of such update. Please visit your nearest branch.

prompt: What are the penalties or operational freezes applied if a customer fails to complete their periodic Re-KYC verification profile?
chosen: The bank sends pre-intimations 6 months before your Re-KYC is due. If it remains incomplete 90 days after the due date, a temporary transactional freeze is applied. Continued non-compliance results in permanent card closure and the foreclosure of all active EMIs.
rejected: ReKYC Master Direction terms say Bank will send 6-month pre intimation to complete the Re-kyc process. If customer does not complete within the given timeline, after 90 days from due date, temporary freeze will be applied. If customer fails to complete, credit card will be closed permanently.

prompt: What are the plan options and monthly subscription fees for the Build Your Own Card (BYOC) Credit Card feature?
chosen: The BYOC card offers choices through three optional subscription tiers: the Silver Plan at INR 99 (choose any 2 of 5 merchants), the Gold Plan at INR 149 (choose any 3 of 5 merchants), and the Platinum Plan at INR 249 (access benefits across all 5 participating merchants).
rejected: BYOC Subscription Details are: Silver Plan (INR 99) – Cardmember can choose any 2 of the 5 merchants, Gold Plan (INR 149) - Cardmember can choose any 3 of the 5 merchants, Platinum Plan (INR 249) - Cardmember can avail benefits from all the 5 merchants. Participating Merchants: Swiggy, Uber, PharmEasy, Bigbasket, Bookmyshow.

prompt: What is the absolute maximum cashback limit a user can earn per statement cycle on a BYOC Credit Card line?
chosen: Cashback on the BYOC card is capped at INR 100 per merchant per statement cycle, with an absolute overall ceiling of INR 1,000 per statement cycle. Utility and wallet spending are completely excluded from earning cashback.
rejected: BYOC Cashback Cappings state: Maximum cashback is INR 100 per customer per statement cycle for every merchant. Maximum Cashback capping of INR 1000 per statement cycle applicable on BYOC Cashback card. No Cashback to be earned on Utility & Wallet Spends for BYOC Cashback card.

prompt: Explain the order of priority or payment hierarchy when the bank appropriates an inward payment credit against card outstandings.
chosen: Inward card payments are adjusted in the following strict order of priority: GST first, followed by TCS, membership or rental/wallet fees, active EMI amounts, other fees or service charges, interest (finance charges), and finally, your core principal balance.
rejected: Payment Allocation Priority: Payments received against the Cardmember’s card outstanding will be adjusted against: GST, TCS, Membership/Rental/Wallet fee, EMI amount, other fees /Charges, Interest (Financial Charges), and Principal in that order. This is handled automatically by systems.

prompt: How many calendar days do cardholders have to report billing discrepancies or statement disputes before the charges are deemed accepted?
chosen: Cardholders must notify the bank of any statement discrepancies or billing disputes within 10 working days of the statement generation date. After this window, all billed items are deemed correct and fully accepted.
rejected: Statement/ Billing Disputes terms: All the billed charges and content of the statement will deem to be correct and accepted, if the Cardmember does not inform YES BANK of the discrepancies (if any) within 10 working days from the statement date. Don't delay checking it.

prompt: What accounting methodology is used by the bank to calculate the exact number of overdue days for SMA or NPA account classification?
chosen: YES Bank utilizes the First In, First Out (FIFO) accounting method to determine overdue timelines for account stress tracking. This approach assumes that inward payments must automatically clear the oldest outstanding dues in the card account first.
rejected: Account Classification Principles: The Principle of FIFO i.e., ‘First In, First Out’ accounting method is relevant to arrive at the No.of days of overdue for determining the SMA/NPA status. The FIFO principle assumes that the oldest outstanding dues in the loan account/ Credit Card needs to be cleared first.

prompt: What are the timeline parameters for an account to move from standard to Special Mention Account (SMA-1 or SMA-2) status due to continuous payment defaults?
chosen: An account is classified as SMA-0 if the principal or interest balance is overdue for up to 30 days. It moves to SMA-1 status for defaults lasting between 31 and 60 days, and escalates to SMA-2 if the payment default continues for 61 to 90 days.
rejected: SMA Sub-categories Matrix states: - SMA-0: Principal or interest payment completely or partly overdue up to 30 days - SMA-1: Overdue period from more than 30 days and up to 60 days - SMA-2: Overdue period from more than 60 days and up to 90 days. Keep your accounts clear to avoid these.
